ksa_text: Skill in establishing and facilitating data and AI communities of practice, including knowledge-sharing forums, best-practice documentation, cross-team collaboration initiatives, and capability-building activities.

dcwf.top_element_label: Facilitate cross-sharing of best practices for data usage.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Operations & Program Management is a primary or secondary domain for the Director of Data Products. This skill KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Operations & Program Management), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.527). External reviewers may question whether this specific skill KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Products versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: PMI, TOGAF operational frameworks, and CDO survey data (NewVantage Partners) confirm operational program management as a key competency for data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Operations & Program Management appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Data Products.

decision_rationale: Operations & Program Management skill KSA is within scope for the Director of Data Products at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
